mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE jumps exceed 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, driving positive equity returns over a quarter as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 60 days (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, forcing equity repricing and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods yields positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of cases, or regime-conditional IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 5% triggers aggressive central bank hawkish guidance expectations, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 248.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates robust labor absorption, supporting consumer spending and generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a negative mean 60-day return or a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate revenue forecasts, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns (p > 0.10) across multiple rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in over 65% of instances following a >0.5% monthly drop in INDPRO during recovery phases.
